title: Inconsistent responses for linked_item_ids in connectboard and dependency column values
excerpt:
Hi there, 
I noticed and inconsistency in how the linked_item_ids are delivered in connect board columns and in dependency columns.

posts.author: kranthi_thoughtflow (Kranthi Kiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
I noticed and inconsistency in how the linked_item_ids are delivered in connect board columns and in dependency columns.

Here is an example of the query

image393×542 6.73 KB

Here is a sample of the response for connect board column value

And the response for dependency column value

image691×381 7.72 KB

As you can see the connectedItemIds is an array of strings where as the dependentItemIds is a stringified version of an array.

Ideally, I would have expected both of these to send a similar data type.

Wanted to check if this is the expected behavior or a bug. Or something that might change in the future.

Regards.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @kranthi_thoughtflow,

Thank you for the feedback!

This is not a bug. It could change in the future though. I have turned this topic into a request.

Everyone: You can vote for this request at the top of the screen!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Matias.Monday I already reported this. It is a bug because the responsev for linked_item_ids in DependencyValue is a serialized array of INT and does not conform to the published schema which says a single ID!. The published schema doesn’t conform to the actual data (which should be an [ID!]) either.

Support confirmed the devs will address this in a future version.

UPDATE: Bug/Schema non-conformance in ... on DependencyValue monday Apps & Developers
Here is the example query. { items(ids: "123123123") { column_values { ... on BoardRelationValue { linked_item_ids } ... on DependencyValue { linked_item_ids } } } } There is a problem here because according to the schema linked_item_ids on BoardRelationValue is of type [ID!] and DependencyValue is ID!. This means the two fragments cannot be used together technically. [image] Although in an practice it works because the two column types ne…
